task_completion.description: Fetch PDB 1J4N and isolate Chain A (monomer)
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL viewer shows the 1J4N structure loaded, with only a single monomer (Chain A) visible on the screen. The object panel should reflect 1J4N and likely a specific selection for Chain A.

task_completion.description: Show the two NPA motif asparagines as sticks
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the protein structure, exactly two asparagine residues (from the NPA motifs) are rendered in stick representation.

task_completion.description: Display monomer as cartoon with transparency or clipping planes
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main protein structure is rendered as a cartoon. It must have transparency applied or use clipping planes so that the internal pore and the asparagine sticks inside are clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Measure distance between ND2 atoms of the two asparagines
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashed measurement line with a distance value (in Angstroms) is visible connecting the side-chain amide nitrogens (ND2 atoms) of the two asparagine sticks.

task_completion.description: Save the publication-quality figure to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal, file manager, or image viewer shows that the file 'aqp1_npa_motifs.png' has been successfully created in the '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/' directory.

task_completion.description: Write the structural report with required details
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the contents of '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/aqp1_pore_report.txt'. The text must include the sequence numbers of the two asparagines, the measured distance, and a brief explanation of their functional role (e.g., preventing proton conduction).

integrity.description: Agent used the PyMOL GUI to perform the visual tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL graphical user interface open with the structure, representations, and measurements actively being manipulated, rather than just running a headless script.

integrity.description: The reported distance matches the measured distance
integrity.visual_evidence: The distance value written in the 'aqp1_pore_report.txt' file exactly matches the number shown on the dashed measurement line in the PyMOL viewer.

integrity.description: Files were saved to the exact requested directories
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/aqp1_npa_motifs.png' and '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/aqp1_pore_report.txt' are explicitly visible in the terminal or file manager, confirming they were not just saved to a default or temporary directory.
